content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN, and an integer KKK.

Let f(l,r)f(l,r)f(l,r) be defined as the number of **distinct** elements in the subarray A[l,r]A[l, r]A[l,r].

Find the sum of fKf^KfK over all subarrays.

More formally, find ∑l=1n∑r=lnfK(l,r)\sum_{l=1}^{n} \sum_{r=l}^{n} f^K(l,r)∑l=1n​∑r=ln​fK(l,r).

Since this value can be huge, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note:


fK(l,r)f^K(l,r)fK(l,r) denotes the product of KKK copies of f(l,r)f(l,r)f(l,r).
A[l,r]A[l, r]A[l,r] denotes the subarray [Al,A(l+1),…,Ar][A_l, A_{(l+1)}, \ldots, A_r][Al​,A(l+1)​,…,Ar​].

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK - the length of the array AAA and the parameter KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2⋯ ,ANA_1, A_2 \cdots , A_NA1​,A2​⋯,AN​ - the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the value of the expression ∑l=1n∑r=lnfK(l,r)\sum_{l=1}^{n} \sum_{r=l}^{n} f^K(l,r)∑l=1n​∑r=ln​fK(l,r) mod
998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 666 subarrays. The various values of fff are :

f(1,1)=f(2,2)=f(3,3)=1f(1,1) = f(2,2) = f(3,3) = 1f(1,1)=f(2,2)=f(3,3)=1
f(1,2)=f(2,3)=2f(1,2) = f(2,3) = 2f(1,2)=f(2,3)=2
f(1,3)=2f(1,3) = 2f(1,3)=2

Since K=1K = 1K=1, we should sum these values up. The answer is 1+1+1+2+2+2=91 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 91+1+1+2+2+2=9.
Test case 222: The values of fff are the same but we should sum their squares instead. The answer is 12+12+12+22+22+22=151^2 + 1^2 + 1^2 + 2^2 + 2^2 + 2^2 = 1512+12+12+22+22+22=15.
Test case 333: There are 101010 subarrays. The various values of fff are :

f(1,1)=f(2,2)=f(3,3)=f(4,4)=f(1,2)=f(3,4)=1f(1,1) = f(2,2) = f(3,3) = f(4, 4) = f(1, 2) = f(3, 4) = 1f(1,1)=f(2,2)=f(3,3)=f(4,4)=f(1,2)=f(3,4)=1
f(2,3)=f(1,3)=f(2,4)=f(1,4)=2f(2,3) = f(1,3) = f(2,4) = f(1,4) = 2f(2,3)=f(1,3)=f(2,4)=f(1,4)=2

Since K=3K = 3K=3, we should sum the cube of these values. The answer is 6⋅13+4⋅23=386 \cdot 1^3 + 4 \cdot 2^3 = 386⋅13+4⋅23=38.
